title: Tactical Planner
description: Own the S&OE horizon — monitor near-term plans, manage exceptions in real time, and use planning software and ERP to keep supply and demand aligned week by week.

keyAreas: The end-to-end operating model: where S&OE sits between monthly S&OP and daily execution, and why the tactical horizon matters
Who is in the planning team and how the tactical planner connects to demand, supply, and commercial functions
Why data quality matters in the near-term plan — and what poor data costs when managing live exceptions
What ERP is, what planning software is, and how they work together to support tactical decision-making
S&OE fundamentals: the weekly cadence, what triggers exception-based working, and how the integrated review drives decisions
How S&OP outputs set the planning horizon the tactical planner works within
Planning software data objects, the network model, and how demand and supply signals flow through the system
Planning logic: push/pull, safety stock, disaggregation, and how the system nets inventory against demand
ERP data model: how master and transactional data are structured and how they feed into near-term planning
How data flows into and out of ERP and back to planning software — and what the tactical planner depends on
RASCI accountability across S&OE demand, supply, and integrated reviews
Data types and quality standards that make near-term plan data reliable enough to act on
End-to-end system integration: how planning software, ERP, and the wider landscape connect
Running S&OE: demand monitoring, supply monitoring, exception management, and the integrated weekly review
Hands-on planning software: reading the near-term plan, running simulations, and managing exceptions in the tool
ERP workflows: creating and releasing orders, processing goods receipts and issues, and reading execution output
Navigating planning software and ERP side by side — knowing when to act in which system
Applying planning policy in the S&OE window: service level priorities, safety stock, and allocation rules
Governance and escalation: when to surface exceptions and how to resolve them without disrupting the monthly plan
KPI ownership: near-term metrics, exception resolution rates, and how tactical performance feeds S&OP review
Data governance: ownership of near-term plan data and maintaining data quality through the S&OE cycle
Integration discipline: keeping planning software and ERP in sync through the tactical horizon
